articles.full_text:
Rain and dust storm warning in 14 Rajasthan districts:Orange alert for hail tomorrow, cloud hover over Kota and Udaipur divisions
- Copy link
A strong new weather system will become active across Rajasthan from today (Monday), prompting Orange and Yellow alerts for dust storms, rain and hailstorms on Monday and Tuesday. Warnings for dust storms and rain have been issued in 14 districts.
The spell of dust storms and rain that had affected the state over the past few days weakened on Sunday. Light cloud cover persisted in parts of Udaipur and Kota divisions, while the rest of the state experienced dry weather and clear skies, allowing bright sunshine. Temperatures rose by 1 to 2 degrees Celsius in several cities.
Sunny weather in many districts
Over the last 24 hours, skies remained clear and sunny across Jaipur, Ajmer, Bharatpur and Bikaner divisions. The highest maximum temperature recorded was 35.1°C in Barmer. Other recorded maximum temperatures included 34°C in Kota, 33.3°C in Jodhpur, 32.6°C in Phalodi, 32.8°C in Dausa, 32.3°C in Pratapgarh, 32.5°C in Karauli, 32.2°C in Jalore, 33.3°C in Dungarpur, 32.4°C in Baran, 32.9°C in Jaisalmer, 32.1°C in Churu, 31.4°C in Bikaner, 31.1°C in Sri Ganganagar, 31.8°C in Udaipur, 31.2°C in Ajmer and 32°C in Alwar.
On Sunday, light cloud cover was observed from morning until afternoon across Kota and Udaipur divisions. By the afternoon, skies gradually cleared, allowing brief sunshine. Later in the evening, light clouds reappeared over Jalore, Sirohi and Barmer areas, though no rainfall was recorded.
New weather system from April 6
According to Radheshyam Sharma, Director of the Meteorological Centre in Jaipur, a strong new weather system will become active from April 6. Its impact is expected to bring significant changes, particularly in Jaisalmer, Jodhpur, Barmer, Bikaner and Sri Ganganagar regions after noon. Cloudy skies are likely, along with dust storms and the possibility of light to moderate rainfall accompanied by hail in several areas.
Peak impact and outlook
The system is expected to reach peak intensity on April 7, when cloud cover may extend across the entire state, with rainfall likely in most regions. Its influence is expected to subside from April 9, after which dry weather conditions are likely to return across Rajasthan.
